title: asplenium subspathulinum
intro: Asplenium subspathulinum, also known as the maidenhair spleenwort, is a type of fern that belongs to the Aspleniaceae family. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain, making it a valuable treatment for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Asplenium subspathulinum are often dried and used to make teas or infusions, which can be used to treat ailments such as fever, coughs, and skin irritations.
